footer.tagline: International film festival for young audiences · Rome

manifesto.body: Alice nella Città is the autonomous parallel section of the Rome Film Festival dedicated to young audiences and new generations. For over twenty years, an open laboratory on cinema, its forms and its protagonists.

chi_siamo.label: 01 — About
chi_siamo.card1_body: We believe in reversing the festival's boundaries: not just premieres, but interdependence between people, ideas and shared practices. A festival that ties together cinema, city and new generations.
chi_siamo.card2_body_html: The festival is conceived and directed by Gianluca Giannelli and Fabia Bettini, who since 2002 have led a cultural project dedicated to cinema for young audiences.

items.title: Competition
items.desc: International premieres in the running for the Alice award.

items.title: Sintonie
items.desc: A programme line shared with Alberto Barbera, director of the Venice Film Festival.

items.title: Onde Corte
items.desc: Short films and Academy for emerging young directors.

items.title: Fuori Sala
items.desc: Cinema beyond the theatres, inside the city's places.

film.label: 03 — Now showing
film.intro: Twelve titles from the XXIV edition. Premieres, first features, restorations and special screenings.

items.label: Masterclass
items.title: Kenneth Lonergan
items.desc: A conversation with the Oscar-winning director of Manchester by the Sea, an essential voice in American independent cinema.

items.label: Meeting
items.title: The filmmakers of Anemone
items.desc: A conversation with the authors of Anemone, the first feature of their generation.

items.label: Anniversary
items.title: Footloose Celebration
items.desc: A special tribute dedicated to an icon of generational cinema.

biglietti_section.label: 05 — Tickets & Accreditation
biglietti_section.intro: Buy tickets for screenings, choose a pass for the entire duration of the festival, or request a professional accreditation.

card_biglietti.label: A — Tickets
card_biglietti.body: Purchase single tickets, passes and festival passes through the festival's official box office. Sales open in October 2026.

card_accrediti.label: B — Accreditation
card_accrediti.body: Accreditations for press, industry, schools and trade operators. All information and procedures to attend the festival professionally.

partner.label: 06 — Partners
partner.intro: Alice nella Città is made possible thanks to the contribution and partnership of institutions, cultural organisations and media partners that share our commitment to cinema for new generations.

newsletter.label: 08 — Newsletter
newsletter.intro: Previews, invites to special events and the festival programme in advance, straight to your inbox.
newsletter.intro_success: Thanks for subscribing. You'll receive the first updates of the XXIV edition soon — see you in October.
newsletter.error: Please enter a valid email address.

page_chi_siamo.lead: For over twenty years, Alice nella Città has been telling the present through the eyes of young people, giving space to those who love cinema and to stories still waiting to be seen.

sections.title: The manifesto.
sections.body: We believe in reversing the festival's boundaries: not only the pursuit of world premieres, but the interdependence between people, ideas and shared practices. A festival that ties together cinema, city and new generations.

sections.title: The Sintonie section.
sections.body: A programme line shared with Alberto Barbera, director of the Venice International Film Festival. A bridge between two festivals, an ongoing dialogue on auteur cinema.

sections.title: Beyond the theatre walls.
sections.body: We experiment with new spaces for cinema: screenings on building facades, events at the Auditorium della Nuvola in partnership with EUR Spa, and the new Fuori Sala format that brings films into the city's places.

sections.title: The founders.
sections.body_html: The festival is conceived and directed by Gianluca Giannelli and Fabia Bettini, who for over twenty years have led a cultural project dedicated to cinema for young audiences.

page_programma.film_label: A selection of films in the programme

sections.title: Competition
sections.description: Premieres and first screenings of international feature films competing for the Alice award.

sections.title: Opening Film
sections.description: The film that opens the festival, chosen as the manifesto of the edition.

sections.title: Out of Competition
sections.description: A selection of must-see works presented outside the official competition.

sections.title: Panorama Italia
sections.description: The best of new Italian cinema for young audiences, with the Audience Award.

sections.title: Sintonie
sections.description: Programme line shared with Alberto Barbera, director of the Venice Film Festival.

sections.title: Onde Corte — Competition
sections.description: International short films in competition from around the world.

sections.title: Onde Corte — Academy
sections.description: A space dedicated to emerging young directors and training.

sections.title: Shorts Without Borders
sections.description: Short films crossing borders, languages and generations.

sections.title: Quei Ragazzi
sections.description: A section dedicated to the storytelling of adolescence and its protagonists.

sections.title: Series
sections.description: The best series productions in preview, dedicated to a young audience.

sections.title: Tributes
sections.description: Tributes and retrospectives to filmmakers, actors and works that made history.

sections.title: Special Events
sections.description: Masterclasses, meetings with filmmakers and joint celebrations.

sections.title: Special Screenings
sections.description: Event-screenings open to the city's public, beyond the official calendar.

sections.title: Fuori Sala
sections.description: The festival's new format: cinema leaves the theatres and reaches Rome's places.

page_biglietti.lead: Everything you need to attend the XXIV edition: tickets, passes, reductions and professional accreditations.

ticket_types.name: Single ticket
ticket_types.desc: For each single screening of the festival.

ticket_types.name: Festival Pass
ticket_types.desc: Unlimited access to all screenings of the edition.

ticket_types.name: Weekend Pass
ticket_types.desc: Access to all screenings of the weekend.

page_biglietti.cta_band_text: Buy your tickets through the official online box office.

accrediti.name: Press
accrediti.label: Press
accrediti.desc: Reserved for journalists, photographers, bloggers and media operators. Free accreditation upon presentation of press credentials.

accrediti.name: Industry
accrediti.label: Professional
accrediti.desc: For producers, distributors, authors, agents and audiovisual operators. Professional rate accreditation.

accrediti.name: Schools
accrediti.label: Education
accrediti.desc: Packages for classes with morning screenings and workshops. Registration through the referring teacher.

news_detail.not_found_body: The article you are looking for is no longer available or the link is invalid.

items.title: «Good Boy» opens the XXIII edition
items.excerpt: Ben Leonberg's debut horror — told entirely from a dog's perspective — opens the XXIII edition of Alice in the City. After premiering at SXSW, the film went viral, hitting 95% on Rotten Tomatoes.

items.title: Fuori Sala: the new format created by Alice in the City
items.excerpt: Fuori Sala takes the festival beyond screenings: cinema, literature and women's empowerment across October and early November, with Robert De Niro arriving in Rome to close the line-up.

items.title: Apply for the 2025 Jury pre-selection
items.excerpt: Applications are open for Italian high-school students who want to join the Alice in the City jury. Submit a film review and the required forms by 18 September 2025.
